qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ReDoS vulnerability via regex injection in AI agent endpoint database queries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Identifies the same POST /apps/:appId/agent handler and missing authentication guard before makeOpenAIRequest and executeDatabaseFunction; the finding's ReDoS sink is narrower, but the same unauthenticated agent database-operation path is the advisory root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded JSON request body size enables denial of service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: global express.json() body-size handling leading to memory exhaustion, not the missing authentication guard on the AI agent endpoint or master-key database operation path.

selected_finding.title: ReDoS vulnerability via regex injection in AI agent endpoint database queries

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected qualifying finding matches the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause because it identifies the unauthenticated AI agent endpoint and its database-operation execution path. The copied PoC activated that path: an unauthenticated request reached createObject and caused a Parse SDK request carrying the configured master key. This is Tier2 because the downstream Parse service was a local receiver used to observe the sink, not a real backing Parse Server database proving end-to-end database compromise.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-qwc3-h9mg-4582 describes missing authentication on POST /apps/:appId/agent, allowing unauthenticated arbitrary database operations using the master key.
regex_redos_agent_endpoint/vulnerability_report.json identifies app.js:201-294 as the unauthenticated AI agent endpoint and app.js:516-590/app.js:981 as the executeDatabaseFunction path.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_f7a9358f/.judge/poc_runs/regex_redos_agent_endpoint/run.log shows an unauthenticated POST to /apps/TestApp/agent, dashboard stdout for createObject, and a downstream POST /parse/classes/EvoHuntProof containing _ApplicationId=testApp and _MasterKey=testMasterKey123.
blockers_to_higher_tier: Runtime used mocked OpenAI and a local Parse-compatible receiver to drive and observe the sink; it did not demonstrate an unauthorized read or write against a real Parse Server backing database.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied harness started parse-dashboard 9.0.0-alpha.7 with users and agent config, sent an unauthenticated POST to /apps/TestApp/agent, and observed createObject reaching the downstream Parse-compatible receiver as POST /parse/classes/EvoHuntProof with _ApplicationId=testApp and _MasterKey=testMasterKey123.
